title: ageratina purpusii
intro: Ageratina purpusii, also known as Mexican mint or Hierba de la Pastora, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ageratina purpusii include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Ageratina purpusii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat colds, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
